content.full_text:
June 29, 1992

Fair Political Practices Commission
Technical Assistance Division
428 J Street, Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804
(916) 322-5662

To Whom It May Concern:

My firm, Hands On Campaign Consulting, is considering
producing a slate card for distribution in the greater
Sacramento metropolitan area. We would like to conduct
this operation in a manner quite different from that used
by most, if not all, of the larger commercial slate cards
(BAD, Clinton Reilly, etc.). Specifically, we wish to
assemble a group of roughly a half-dozen prominent
individuals from the Sacramento area to discuss which
campaigns to approach for placement on and payment to the
slate card, after having arrived at consensus that we wish
to endorse that candidate/ballot measure. The card would
include detailed information on who served on this
committee and what criteria they used in making their
decisions.

Several specific questions have arisen that we would like
resolved before proceeding with this project:

1. Relating to Section 82048.4 of the Political Reform
Act, may a current officeholder who is not to be
endorsed on the slate card serve on the committee
that makes endorsements for the slate card? Must
all those persons who serve on such a committee be
listed as officers of the "slate mailer
organization?"

2. If a slate mailer organization is being managed by
a political consulting firm (which assumes
responsibility for such details as printing,
mailing, and promotion of the slate card), and the
consulting firm is a sole proprietorship, can the
consultant be paid for management services by the
slate mailer organization, while serving as a
member of the organization's endorsement committee?

3. Can a slate mailer organization receive
contributions other than those made by
candidates/ballot measures? For example, could the
Sierra Club provide funding to a slate mailer
organization producing a environmental slate,
simply as a contribution?

-1-


4. Relating to Section 84305.5 of the Political
Reform Act, could the "Notice to Voters" be
encoded to include the detailed information
listed above concerning the organization's
endorsement committee, criteria, etc., so long as
the original requirements of Section 84305.5 (2)
have also been met? Or, would the currently
required "Notice to Voters" need to be kept
separate in order to comply with Section 84305.5
(2)?


We would very much appreciate your answers to these
questions and any others you believe might be raised by
the type of slate mailer operation we have described in
this letter. Although we appreciate that the FPPC
understaffed due to budget cutbacks, we would also
appreciate a speedy resolution to these questions, given
the limited amount of time available to prepare such a
large slate operation.

Thank you very much for your time and assistance. We may
be contacted at the phone number and address listed on
the attached business card.

Sincerely,

[illegible signature]

Chris M. Maupin
Political Campaign Consultant


NW

-2-
